reforms.description: The Austrian government has initiated a 'technology offensive' to enhance technological diffusion and strengthen linkages between public and private research. This initiative aims to increase R&D expenditures, particularly among small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs), and is linked to a broader strategy to stimulate exports.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By enhancing linkages between public and private sectors and increasing R&D expenditures, the reform is expected to raise productivity and innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The technology offensive has been launched and is currently in force.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform represents a significant structural change aimed at enhancing R&D and technological diffusion.
reforms.source_quote: The government has decided a programme ('technology offensive', Technologieoffensive) to strengthen the linkages between public and private research and to enhance the development of skills within industry.

reforms.description: Austria has recognized the need to support an entrepreneurial climate by fostering access to venture capital markets and easing administrative requirements for creating new businesses. These measures aim to facilitate the establishment and growth of innovative firms.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By improving access to venture capital and reducing barriers to starting new businesses, the reform is expected to enhance innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has acknowledged the necessity of these reforms but they have not yet been implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reforms are significant in promoting entrepreneurship but do not represent a structural overhaul.
reforms.source_quote: Supporting an entrepreneurial climate and enhancing creation and diffusion of technological know-how: access to venture capital markets needs to be fostered.

reforms.description: The government has introduced a technology package aimed at promoting basic research and increasing public sector R&D, alongside subsidies for employing scientists in industry. This initiative seeks to enhance the overall research capacity within the country.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By promoting basic research and providing subsidies for employing scientists, the reform is expected to enhance innovation capacity and productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The technology package has been introduced but is not yet implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform is significant in promoting public sector R&D but does not represent a major structural change.
reforms.source_quote: Technology package still being discussed which seeks to raise level of public sector R&D.
